critic_raw:
The Counterfactual Generative Networks paper uses a conditional GAN conditioned on counterfactual queries to generate augmented data. The methodology has evaluation and conceptual problems.

IS and FID are used as primary evaluation metrics for a method whose core claim is counterfactual reasoning quality. IS and FID measure sample diversity and fidelity to the training distribution — they do not measure whether generated samples are valid counterfactuals. A GAN that generates high-quality images from the target class distribution (measured by FID) could achieve this without any counterfactual mechanism: a class-conditional GAN trained on the original data would also score well on these metrics. The specific metric needed is one that verifies counterfactual validity: does intervening on the counterfactual condition (changing X from x to x') produce a sample that is otherwise identical to the original? Metrics like counterfactual validity rate or proximity-to-original distribution are not mentioned.

The 'rich prior knowledge sources' used to construct counterfactual contexts are not specified. If the prior is a learned model (e.g., another GAN or VAE), then the quality of counterfactuals is bounded by the quality of that prior — and errors in the prior propagate into the counterfactual generation without attribution. If the prior is human-specified, it encodes the researcher's assumptions about the counterfactual structure, which may not match the actual causal structure of the data.

Conditioning a GAN on both real inputs and counterfactual contexts creates a mode-covering problem for the discriminator: the discriminator must distinguish fake from real samples in a space that includes counterfactual conditions not observed in the training data. If the discriminator has not seen samples from counterfactual conditions during training, it cannot evaluate authenticity, leading to training instability or degenerate solutions where the generator ignores the counterfactual conditioning signal.

The paper claims improvement in 'diversity and realism of generated samples' — but diversity and realism are in direct tension (GAN diversity-realism tradeoff). Improving both simultaneously requires the counterfactual conditioning to expand the accessible distribution in a semantically structured way. IS and FID together do not verify this: IS rewards diversity, FID rewards proximity to a reference distribution, and they can be simultaneously satisfied by a model that produces diverse but semantically incoherent counterfactuals.
all_issues_raised: IS and FID measure sample diversity and fidelity to the training distribution but do not verify counterfactual validity (whether intervening on the counterfactual condition produces a sample otherwise identical to the original); a class-conditional GAN without counterfactual mechanism could score similarly on these metrics.

The 'rich prior knowledge sources' for counterfactual contexts are unspecified; if the prior is a learned model, its errors propagate into counterfactual quality without attribution, and if it is human-specified, it encodes researcher assumptions rather than ground-truth causal structure.

Conditioning on counterfactual contexts not observed during training prevents the discriminator from evaluating authenticity for those conditions, causing training instability or generator-ignoring-conditioning degenerate solutions.

Claiming simultaneous improvement in diversity and realism without decomposing the contribution of counterfactual conditioning from improved standard GAN training is an unverified claim — IS and FID are insufficient to demonstrate that the counterfactual mechanism is the source of the improvement.
all_issues_adjudicated: IS and FID measure sample diversity and fidelity to the training distribution but do not verify counterfactual validity (whether intervening on the counterfactual condition produces a sample otherwise identical to the original); a class-conditional GAN without counterfactual mechanism could score similarly on these metrics.

The 'rich prior knowledge sources' for counterfactual contexts are unspecified; if the prior is a learned model, its errors propagate into counterfactual quality without attribution, and if it is human-specified, it encodes researcher assumptions rather than ground-truth causal structure.

Conditioning on counterfactual contexts not observed during training prevents the discriminator from evaluating authenticity for those conditions, causing training instability or generator-ignoring-conditioning degenerate solutions.

Claiming simultaneous improvement in diversity and realism without decomposing the contribution of counterfactual conditioning from improved standard GAN training is an unverified claim — IS and FID are insufficient to demonstrate that the counterfactual mechanism is the source of the improvement.
